# Lumecon Inc.

> Lumecon Inc. is economic and policy analysis software for governments and mission-driven organizations. A modern alternative to IMPLAN, RIMS II, REMI, and BEA RIMS multipliers. Built at Cornell University. Parent brand of three specialized platforms: Local Economic Impact, Tribal Economic Impact, and Global Economic Impact. Cedar is the built-in AI assistant that reads messy data across departments, surfaces assumptions, and writes the source record for each run.

Lumecon replaces both the consultant and the overpriced legacy software in your budget process. It runs impact studies in-house on your own real-time administrative data using current modeling methods, and ships each run with the full assumption ledger, sources, and method so a council member or grant reviewer can verify the result. We use AI to reduce the infrastructure and analysis constraints organizations face, not as a marketing gimmick.

## Category

Economic impact analysis software. Direct alternatives include IMPLAN, RIMS II (Bureau of Economic Analysis), REMI Policy Insight, and BEA RIMS II multipliers. Differentiators: built-in AI for handling messy administrative data, native AIANNH (American Indian/Alaska Native/Native Hawaiian) geography, transparent multipliers with full source documentation, and a price point accessible to small governments and mission-driven organizations.

## Platforms

- [Local Economic Impact](https://localeconomicimpact.com/): For US municipal governments, economic development organizations, enterprises, and nonprofits. In active development.
- [Tribal Economic Impact](https://tribaleconomicimpact.com/): For tribes, Alaska Native Corporations, Native Hawaiian Organizations, and reservation economies, with Indigenous data sovereignty in the foundation. Patent pending.
- [Global Economic Impact](https://globaleconomicimpact.com/): For multinationals, NGOs, and governments. Cross-border and multiregional analysis. Future development.

## Cedar

Cedar is the AI assistant inside every Lumecon platform. It reads user uploads (budgets, payroll detail, council agendas, chapter audits, federal grant filings), surfaces each assumption the model is about to use, prompts the user to confirm or override, and writes a record of every source, method, and multiplier as the run completes. Marketing-page Cedar is scoped to questions about the demo and does not call any upstream provider.

## Audience

Governments and mission-driven organizations: municipal governments, tribal nations and tribal enterprises, state agencies, federal programs, universities and research labs, foundations, Community Development Financial Institutions (CDFIs), economic development organizations.

## Concepts the site covers

Economic impact analysis, input-output modeling, direct effects, indirect effects, induced effects, fiscal multipliers, jobs supported, output, earnings, value added, IMPLAN, RIMS II, REMI, BEA RIMS, tribal economics, reservation economics, AIANNH geography, ANCSA regional corporations, CDFI lending, foundation reporting, federal grant pass-through, economic development organizations, public-sector analytics.

## Origin

Built at Cornell University with counsel from the Cornell Law Entrepreneurship Law Clinic. Founder's academic background spans Cornell and Dartmouth (Ivy League).
